name: Tamarind-Glazed Pork Chops with Mole Cream
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Roasted, peeled, and chopped Poblano pepper
4 tbsp Dark cane or corn syrup
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F.
2. In a food processor, combine pumpkin seeds, pistachio nuts, pine nuts, 3 tbsp tamarind paste, roasted Poblano pepper, chili powder, cumin, salt, dark cane syrup, vinegar, and olive oil. Puree until creamy, stopping occasionally to scrape the sides of the bowl.
3. Pour the sauce into a saucepan. Whisk in chicken stock and heavy cream. Bring the mixture to a boil and reduce to a simmer. Cook for 2 minutes. Remove from heat and keep warm.
4. In another food processor, puree remaining tamarind paste, garlic, dark cane syrup, molasses, ketchup, water, black pepper, and Essence. Puree until pasty.
5. Season each pork chop with Essence.
6. Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a large sauté pan. When the oil is hot, add the chops and sear for 5 minutes on each side and an additional 2 minutes on the fat edge.
7. Place the pan in the oven and roast the chops for about 25 minutes. Remove from the oven and let rest for 5 minutes.
8. Assemble by spooning sweet potatoes in the center of each plate. Lay each chop in the center of the potatoes. Spoon mole sauce over each chop. Drizzle tamarind paste over each chop.
